probable_causes: Erosion due to high-velocity flue gas impingement
Corrosion from high sulfur content in coal
Overheating due to internal scale deposit or flow blockage
Weld joint failure from thermal fatigue cycling
Fly ash erosion at tube bends and penetration areas
diagnostic_procedures: Perform ultrasonic thickness measurement on affected tubes
Conduct visual inspection for bulging, discoloration, or pinhole marks
Check boiler water chemistry — pH, dissolved oxygen, phosphate levels
Review tube metal temperature trends from thermocouples
Analyze failed tube sample for metallurgical root cause
corrective_actions: Replace affected tube section with matching grade material
Install erosion-resistant shields or baffles at high-wear zones
Perform hydrostatic test after tube replacement to confirm integrity
Normalize weld joints post-repair per ASME standards
preventive_actions: Schedule periodic UT thickness surveys during planned outages
Maintain boiler water chemistry within BHEL recommended limits
Install erosion monitoring probes at known high-erosion zones
Review and optimize soot blower operation sequence
safety_precautions: Ensure complete boiler shutdown and depressurization before entry
Follow confined space entry procedures for drum/header inspection
Verify scaffolding integrity before tube repair at elevation

probable_causes: Long-term overheating due to steam temperature excursion
Short-term overheating from steam flow starvation
Fireside corrosion from alkali metal deposits
diagnostic_procedures: Check steam temperature records for excursions beyond design limits
Inspect tube external surface for thick oxide scale or wastage
Perform creep damage assessment on high-temperature sections
Review attemperator spray operation and control logic
corrective_actions: Replace failed tube coil with upgraded material (T91/T22 as applicable)
Clean internal deposits by chemical cleaning if scale buildup detected
preventive_actions: Implement oxide scale thickness monitoring program
Review and limit unit cycling to reduce thermal fatigue
Ensure attemperator spray systems are regularly tested
safety_precautions: Ensure steam lines are fully drained and depressurized before work
Use proper PPE for high-temperature zone inspections

probable_causes: Hot air duct leakage reducing drying capacity
Feeder malfunction causing uneven coal feed
diagnostic_procedures: Measure coal fineness at mill outlet (target: 70% through 200 mesh)
Check grinding element wear by visual inspection or measurement
Review mill differential pressure and motor current trends
Inspect reject hopper for oversized particles or foreign material
corrective_actions: Repair or replace damaged classifier assembly
preventive_actions: Track mill power consumption trending for early wear detection
Schedule grinding element replacement based on operating hours
Maintain coal quality within design specifications
safety_precautions: Inert the mill completely before any internal inspection
Follow fire/explosion prevention procedures during mill maintenance
Ensure mill is isolated from hot air and coal supply before entry

probable_causes: Ash deposition and fouling of heating elements
Cold-end corrosion due to acid dew point condensation
Unburnt coal particles depositing and igniting in APH
diagnostic_procedures: Check gas outlet temperature for signs of choking
Inspect heating elements for ash buildup during shutdown
corrective_actions: Water wash the APH during planned shutdown if severely fouled
Replace corroded cold-end baskets with enamel-coated elements
Adjust combustion to minimize unburnt carbon in flue gas
preventive_actions: Optimize soot blower sequence and frequency for APH
Maintain flue gas temperature above acid dew point at cold end
Install online APH washing system if fouling is chronic
safety_precautions: In case of APH fire, follow emergency firefighting procedure immediately
Ensure fire detection system is operational before unit startup
